---
title: "Pandoc Layouts Test"
subtitle: "Testing all 7 PowerPoint Slide Layouts"
author: "Antigravity & David"
date: today
date-format: long
format:
  pptx:
    reference-doc: test.pptx
    slide-level: 2
filters:
  - size_tags.lua
---

# Section Header Layout

This is a Level 1 header (`#`), which is above the slide-level of 2 (`##`). Pandoc uses the **Section Header** layout for this slide.

## Title and Content Layout

This slide triggers the **Title and Content** layout. It is a standard slide containing only text and lists, and does not match the criteria for any other layout.

- Standard slide title (`##`)
- Standard bullet points:
  - Bullet point A
  - Bullet point B
  - Bullet point C

## Two Content Layout

This slide triggers the **Two Content** layout. It has two columns, and neither column contains text followed by non-text.

::: {.columns}
::: {.column}
### Left Column

This is the left column containing only text.

- Bullet 1
- Bullet 2
:::

::: {.column}
### Right Column

This is the right column also containing only text.

- Bullet 3
- Bullet 4
:::
:::

## Comparison Layout

This slide triggers the **Comparison** layout. It is a two-column slide where the left column contains text followed by an image (non-text).

::: {.columns}
::: {.column}
Here is some text in the left column.

![](test_image.png)
:::

::: {.column}
Here is the right column content.

- Bullet A
- Bullet B
:::
:::

## Content with Caption Layout

This slide triggers the **Content with Caption** layout because it is a single-column slide containing text followed by an image.

Here is the caption text explaining the image below:

![](test_image.png)

## 

::: {.notes}
This slide has no title and only contains speaker notes. This triggers the **Blank** layout since there is no visible slide content.
:::

## 

\ 
<!-- The slide above has no title and only contains a non-breaking space, which also triggers the **Blank** layout. -->

# Section Header mit minimalem Text

Ein einzelner kurzer Satz unter der Überschrift.

# Section Header ohne Text

## Minimal Two Content (mit kurzem Satz oben)

Ein kurzer Satz oben auf dieser Folie.

::: {.columns}
::: {.column}
Text links.
:::

::: {.column}
Text rechts.
:::
:::

## Minimal Two Content (ohne Satz oben)

::: {.columns}
::: {.column}
Nur Text links.
:::

::: {.column}
Nur Text rechts.
:::
:::

## Minimal Comparison (mit kurzem Satz oben)

Ein kurzer Satz oben auf dieser Folie.

::: {.columns}
::: {.column}
Text links.

![](test_image.png)
:::

::: {.column}
Text rechts.
:::
:::

## Minimal Comparison (ohne Satz oben)

::: {.columns}
::: {.column}
Text links.

![](test_image.png)
:::

::: {.column}
Text rechts.
:::
:::

# Script Test: Section Header Small

::: {.notes}
[layout: Section Header Small]
:::

## Script Test: Title and Content Normal

This is a normal slide with standard font sizes.

- Point 1
- Point 2

## Script Test: Title and Content Small

This slide should have smaller font sizes, mapped by the python script.

- Point 1
- Point 2

::: {.notes}
[layout: Title and Content Small]
:::

## Script Test: Two Content Normal

::: {.columns}
::: {.column}
Normal Text Left
:::
::: {.column}
Normal Text Right
:::
:::

## Script Test: Two Content Small

::: {.columns}
::: {.column}
Smaller Text Left
:::
::: {.column}
Smaller Text Right
:::
:::

::: {.notes}
[layout: Two Content Small]
:::

## Lua Filter Test: Normal Size

Dieser Text ist in der ganz normalen Standardgröße.
Wir verwenden keine speziellen Klassen.

## Lua Filter Test: Smaller {.smaller}

Diese Folie hat das Suffix `{.smaller}` in der Überschrift.
Der Lua-Filter sollte dies erkennen und das Layout auf die `-6pt` Variante verkleinern.

## Lua Filter Test: Smallest {.smallest}

Diese Folie nutzt die `{.smallest}` Klasse.
Sie sollte automatisch das `-12pt` Layout erhalten! Das ist extrem simpel in der Anwendung.
